words: KAREN PURTEE: Okay, start with your family and then maybe how you got here
why.
HILDA RUBERG: Well, my father had been out here during the summer and he bought
this farm out here in Big Meadow and then in November, he brought the
whole family out. There were eleven children at that time and we
landed in Troy on November the 12th- no- what was it?
HELENA C. CARLSON: August.

speaker: HR
words: Huh?

speaker: HCC
words: It was in August. No, it was November, 1912. And so when we got here it was at

speaker: HCC
words: night

speaker: HCC
words: and we had to stay in a hotel down here and in the morning got some

speaker: HCC
words: body to take us out in a hack, you know, and the mud was hub deep

words: in the streets of Troy. And we drove out there, it was three miles

words: out there and")my brother had come up first. He came out with the

words: carload of stock, you know, horses and cattle and so on, so he was

speaker: HCC
words: at home.

speaker: HR
words: in those days, around; we went; to Spokane to do housework. You could always get it.

speaker: HR
words: Troy; bought afarm out from Burnt Ridge and

words: By train?

speaker: HR

words: oldest girl. And then of course, they had the farm, you know, just

speaker: HR
words: like anybody else out here.

words: .We cut wood.

words: haul to town to sell. It was just a farm with just a few acres clea

words: work.

words: a housekeeper?

speaker: HR
words: have Sunday afternoons off, too.

speaker: HR
words: Oh, yes, we lived right in the house. It was kinda fun, you know.

words: know, and oh, I liked it there but I didn't like it as well- I

words: thought if I could, you know. But the backdoor neighbor wanted me

words: over there. She had a girl working for her and she was leaving and

words: she wanted me to come over there to work for her, so I did that.

words: Yeah, they were awful nice to me. did I meet my man? It was

words: at a dance hall! (Laughter) Yeah, isn't that funny? And he^ad

words: Troy relatives. I didn't know that when I met him.

words: neighbors, you know, but I was always the one at home. Had to take

words: and do care of the little kids and helped around, you know. My mother al-

words: help inside. And she worked outside. We were all busy all the time.

words: born after we came out here. And she got burned, you know. Yeah,

speaker: KP
words: the front door because it was hooked, she had to run clear around

speaker: KP
words: the house and the flames was just up above her head, you know, and

speaker: KP

speaker: KP
words: after.

words: in town, so he came out there. And he couldn't do very much for her

words: and her eyebrows singed off and her lips were burned, you know.

speaker: KP

words: Uh-huh.

words: "Well, I guess

words: came down behind

words: months.
